title: oxytropis involuta
intro: Oxytropis involuta, also known as a type of wild pea or locoweed, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Western North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Oxytropis involuta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared by making a decoction from its roots or stems, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically to treat skin conditions.
